dialogos.texto_en: Phew, I was so nervous. As soon as it was over,\nI felt exhausted...
Yeah, I had no idea\nglassmaking was such a difficult task.
All we actually did was blow air into the mold%p-2;――
But still,\nI was nervous and my body was stiff.
Yet, it was a great experience!
Yes!\nWatching our own glass take shape was really enjoyable.
Our own glass...
The only one in the world that I breathed life into.\nBoth the experience and the glass are very precious.
Yeah, I'm looking forward to using it.\nWhen can we receive it?
They will send it by mail once it's completed.\nWe'll have to wait patiently for it.
I hope I can receive it before I leave for Tokyo...
Ah...
Th-that's right.\nTiming-wise, it might be close.
(Our own glass that we made together.\n As I thought%p-2;――%p;)
Hey, Itsuki.\nI thought of something while we were making the glass.
Huh? What is it?
After this trip, I'll be going to Tokyo right away.\nOnce that happens,\nI don't know when we'll see each other again.
Uesugi-kun?\nWhy are you bringing this up all of a sudden?
Sorry, but listen to me.
I thought I was prepared for that,\nbut there was still something I wanted after all.
Something that would make me feel your presence,\nno matter how far apart we are.\nSo%p-2;――
How about we exchange the glass we made with each other?
Until now, we were both focused on our dreams\nand passing exams, and we didn't have time for romance,\nyeah?
We both knew each other's feelings, but...\nour relationship as lovers didn't progress at all,\nand as a result, I made you worried.
However, we've already overcome that.\nLet's take our time and move forward in our own way.
Yeah, you're right.\nI have no worries about that.
Well then%p-2;――
But when it comes to a long-distance relationship,\nthere are different anxieties%p-2;――\n%p;Or rather, loneliness.
...Of course.\nI'm sad...that I'm gonna miss you, Uesugi-kun.
...Ah!\nThat's why you want evidence of that.\nSo that we don't feel lonely even when we're apart.
That's right.
I understand. Let's exchange our glass.
I see! Thanks!
No. I should be thanking you for such a\nwonderful proposal.
This handmade glass will\nmake us feel closer to each other.
Also, it's a memory of this trip.\nI'm sure that every time we use it,\nwe'll remember the stained glass we saw together%p-2;――
I'll feel like you're with me.
...Me too.
(I was actually thinking of kissing her,\n but it's probably better to leave it at this.)
(And besides, even without forcing it,\n my feelings and Itsuki's are certainly connected.)
Hey, Uesugi-kun,\nit's a weird thing to say in return for the glass%p-2;――
I have one more thing to ask you.
